content:
It's a positive thing that net is interested in having rust next to the C files https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/3dc9a1ea-fb04-4b9e-abf9-08165ea3b8c5@lunn.ch/

What would actually be needed to make this possible? At a bare minimum, that crate just needs to be identified, built, and passed as --extern to everything. But then you wind up building net for everything
Which certainly isn't bad when you only have two crates, but would of course need fixing at some point

I guess Miguel answered that on the thread already

This should be solved with the new build system. The plan is to have bindings per subsystem (in your own folder etc.), as well as the abstractions etc.

Does anyone have a link to that build system work?
I don't think the new build system is implemented yet
Is there a PR or repo where it's WIP?
Oh, or is it a new kbuild and not something RFL specific
There's a third version of the patch, so if you have any though about it go and speak it up!

Something interest that I've found is that you can compile a library that only contains reexports and it won't produce symbols, only an empty object and the rmeta. This is useful in case of having various crates living under a subsystem but you want them to be all-in-once.

For example...

// drivers/usb/core/usb_core.rs
pub fn disabled() -> bool {
    bindings::usb_disabled() != 0
}

// drivers/usb/lib.rs
pub use usb_core::disabled;
You can have a crate that does reexports and you don't have to link it to anything, you only require its metadata.
